abstract: Returns a normalized copy of the distinguished name (DN) of the issuer of a certificate.
overview:
## Return Value

A data object containing a DER-encoded X.509 distinguished name suitable for use with [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecItemCopyMatching(_:_:)]. Returns `NULL` if an error occurred. In Objective-C, free the object with a call to the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CoreFoundation/CFRelease] function when you are done with it.

## Discussion

To obtain a copy of the issuer’s distinguished name in a format suitable for display purposes, call [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecCertificateCopyValues(_:_:_:)] instead.

# SecCertificateCopyNormalizedIssuerContent(_:_:)

**Function**

Returns a normalized copy of the distinguished name (DN) of the issuer of a certificate.

## Declaration

```swift
func SecCertificateCopyNormalizedIssuerContent(_ certificate: SecCertificate, _ error: UnsafeMutablePointer<Unmanaged<CFError>?>?) -> CFData?
```

## Parameters

- **certificate**: The certificate from which the issuer’s distinguished name should be copied.
- **error**: A pointer to a [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CoreFoundation/CFError] variable where an error object is stored upon failure. If not `NULL`, the caller is responsible for checking this variable and releasing the resulting object if it exists.

## Return Value

A data object containing a DER-encoded X.509 distinguished name suitable for use with [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecItemCopyMatching(_:_:)]. Returns `NULL` if an error occurred. In Objective-C, free the object with a call to the [doc://com.apple.documentation/documentation/CoreFoundation/CFRelease] function when you are done with it.

## Discussion

To obtain a copy of the issuer’s distinguished name in a format suitable for display purposes, call [doc://com.apple.security/documentation/Security/SecCertificateCopyValues(_:_:_:)] instead.
